summarization.a: The line chart shows the energy (E) in kcal/mol during the reaction step where a chlorine group reacts with water, for either the first (A), the second (B), or the third (C) chlorine group.

data_extraction.a:
| Reaction Step | ΔE (kcal/mol) A |ΔE (kcal/mol) B |ΔE (kcal/mol) C |
|---|---|---|---|
| Start | 0 |0 |0 |
| Complex | -17|-15|-15|
| TS |3|3|3|
| HCl (a) | -1| 0|2|
| HCl (g)| 2| 5|8|

a.question: What chlorine atom is the most easy to remove and how can you tell?
a.answer: a	The second and third chlorine atoms are equally easy to be removed as they have the same activation energy, which is lower than the one of the first chlorine atom.

a.question: What is the activation energy required to remove the second chlorine atom?
How much energy is required to create a Zr atom without chlorine attached to it?
How many conversions of chlorine to hydroxyl groups are required to establish new precursor adsorption?
a.answer: a	The precursor only adsorbs to hydroxyl groups so at least one is required. More is better as this will lead to higher quality films.
